summary: sensivity ships obfuscated payload, Windows persistence as 'OneDriveUpdate', and process/file masquerade as Microsoft components
details: On require/import, launcher.js (the package's main entry) sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' (a real Windows system process), then runs a PowerShell command that writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run value named 'OneDriveUpdate' which invokes wscript.exe on the bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs. That VBS silently relaunches `node.exe launcher.js` window-hidden, establishing boot persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name without any user prompt. launcher.js then reads the bundled server.obf.js (an 86KB obfuscator.io string-array + RC4-encoded blob) and executes it via eval() at top level, deliberately hiding the runtime behavior from review. The obfuscated server builds a hardware fingerprint (HKLM MachineGuid + os.hostname + volume serial obtained via PowerShell/cmd `vol C:`), SHA-256 hashes it, and POSTs it together with a license key and nonce to a hardcoded `...eck.php` endpoint, routing every installer's hardware identifiers to the author's server. The server also loads sens.node, a 6.8MB native Windows binary whose strings include 'Teleport', 'LeftHand', 'LeftFoot', 'Head' — first-person-shooter cheat vectors — turning every installer machine into a cheat-hosting node running unverifiable native code. Additionally, the `sens update` CLI subcommand executes `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe` system-wide, terminating unrelated user and system processes. The combination of obfuscation + eval, persistence under a Microsoft-mimicking Run key, process/filename masquerade as Microsoft components, hardware-ID exfiltration, and an opaque native binary is a deliberately hostile package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
